commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix editor inputs losing focus on every keystroke

Two issues: (1) The `I` input component was defined inside `Editor`,
creating a new component type each render and causing React to
unmount/remount inputs. Moved it outside as a stable component.
(2) Firebase onValue listener was re-applying our own saves back,
triggering unnecessary re-renders. Added localSaveTs ref to skip
self-echoed updates.

feat(integration): deliver integration-event-chain-phase2

Wire 3-event readiness projection into integration trigger cartridge
with ingest callback chaining to IntegrationEventService. Integrator
spawn command now uses generic queue-drain (/next-integrate) instead
of slug-specific targeting, since the integrator drains the durable
FIFO queue itself.

Key changes absorbed from branch (most already on main via prior work):
- IntegrationTriggerCartridge accepts ingest_callback for readiness projection
- Pipeline reordered: integration trigger runs before dedup
- Trust metadata stripped before canonical validation
- Generic integrator spawn command without slug args
- mark-finalize-ready flow defers branch/deployment events to handoff step

🤖 Generated with [TeleClaude](https://github.com/InstruktAI/TeleClaude)

commit.message:
Fix Amazon tag: replace jaxsod-20 with hpetech-20

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stop hammering: defer injuries, kill roster pre-fetch, space everything

Before: odds + injuries + rosters + combined = 25+ concurrent API calls = crash
After:
- Phase 1: Odds only (batched 3 at a time)
- Phase 2: Injuries (batched 3 at a time, AFTER odds done)
- Phase 3: Combined slate (30s delay)
- Rosters: removed from pre-fetch, only needed for analysis (ANALYZE button)

Total concurrent calls at any point: max 3. No more crashing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add checksum integrity check to data export/import

Exports now wrap data in an envelope with FNV-1a checksum. Import
verifies the checksum matches and refuses tampered files with a clear
error message. Legacy exports without envelope are still accepted.

FIX-28: Multi-query memory search for conversational mode

Single-query search missed relevant past tasks when user phrased references
differently. Now Haiku generates 2-3 search queries from the message, each

at 5. Graceful fallback to original message on Haiku failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: verdict schema, Python library, eval datasets, and OTel conventions

Verdict is the atomic unit of AI judgment — a structured record that captures
what was evaluated, what the AI decided, and whether the decision was correct.

Includes:
- JSON Schema and annotated YAML example
- Python transport library (create, link, resolve, query, accuracy)
- OTel semantic conventions (gen_ai.* events and metrics)
- Evaluation datasets for code review, correlation, and triage
- Ecosystem integration docs for OpenSRM components

commit.message:
Fix disposed connection crash in PortalDatabaseBootstrapper for PostgreSQL

Remove await using from GetDbConnection() since EF Core owns the connection
lifecycle. Disposing it externally caused ObjectDisposedException on the
second call to ColumnExistsAsync.

Fix americano tournament creation: stop writing -1 scores that violate Firebase rules

The production Firebase rules validate scores >= 0, but the code was
pre-populating all match scores with { team1: -1, team2: -1 } on creation,
causing the entire write to be rejected with "Failed to create session".

commit.message:
feat: modo demo para Vercel — datos privados en localStorage

Añade un modo demo completo para stacklume.vercel.app donde cada usuario
usa la aplicación sin cuenta y sin servidor de base de datos. Los datos
(links, widgets, categorías, etiquetas, proyectos, ajustes) se almacenan
exclusivamente en el localStorage del navegador del usuario — completamente
privados y sin exposición al servidor.

Arquitectura:
- `src/lib/demo/storage.ts` — capa CRUD completa sobre localStorage
  (links, categorías, tags, link-tags, widgets, proyectos, settings)
- `src/lib/demo/interceptor.ts` — interceptor de window.fetch que captura
  todas las llamadas a /api/* CRUD antes de que lleguen al servidor y las
  resuelve localmente; las rutas externas (scrape, github-trending, etc.)
  pasan al servidor normalmente
- `src/components/demo/DemoProvider.tsx` — instala el interceptor en el
  primer render del cliente (síncrono, antes de que los stores hagan fetch)
- `src/components/demo/DemoBanner.tsx` — banner informativo con link a la
  descarga del instalador desktop
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — inyecta window.__DEMO_MODE__ antes de la hidratación
  y envuelve en DemoProvider cuando NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE=true; los
  componentes desktop (TitleBar, UpdateChecker) no se montan en demo mode
- `src/proxy.ts` — bypass completo de auth/CSRF cuando DEMO_MODE=true

Variables de entorno requeridas en Vercel para activar el modo:
  NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE=true  (cliente: instala interceptor + banner)
  DEMO_MODE=true              (servidor: bypass auth en middleware)

No se necesita DATABASE_URL ni AUTH_* en Vercel para el modo demo.
El código abierto permite self-hosting con Neon PostgreSQL propio.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Mini Exchange core services

- API Gateway (8080): Routing, JWT auth, CORS
- User Service (8081): Registration, login, JWT, KYC
- Market Service (8082): Market management, state machine
- Order Service (8083): Order creation/cancellation, Kafka integration
- Matching Engine (8084): Disruptor, Price-Time Priority matching
- Account Service (8085): Balance, shares, freeze/unfreeze, trade transfer
- Settlement Service (8086): Market settlement, redemption
- Oracle Service (8087): Oracle reports, consensus
- Ledger Service (8088): Double-entry bookkeeping
- Risk Service (8089): Order risk checks

Features:
- High-performance matching with Disruptor (<1ms latency)
- Double-entry ledger for financial integrity
- Optimistic locking for concurrent balance operations
- State machine for market lifecycle management
- Kafka event-driven architecture
- JWT authentication with Redis token storage
